Incomplete deprecation cleanup: pantry-review agent file, Section 2, and RULES.md table rows still structurally present
AGG-034: Fix circular distinct approaches definition in implementation.md
description:
implementation.md defines distinct approaches as requiring different tradeoff profiles, but genuinely different algorithms can share the same tradeoff profile. The definition is circular and confuses Dirt Pushers during design exploration.

Fix: Simplify to concrete criteria: Distinct means different algorithms, OR different data structures, OR different architectural patterns. Cosmetic variations do not count.

Acceptance Criteria:
- [ ] implementation.md defines distinct approaches using concrete criteria, not tradeoff profiles
- [ ] The definition lists specific categories (algorithms, data structures, architectural patterns)
- [ ] A brief example distinguishes distinct from cosmetic variation

title: TIMESTAMP variable undefined in Step 3b-v dummy reviewer
description: Root cause: RULES.md Step 3b-v (line 176) uses ${TIMESTAMP} in a tmux send-keys command, but no prior instruction assigns this value to a variable named TIMESTAMP. Step 3b-i describes generating a timestamp but does not define the variable name. If unset, the dummy reviewer receives a malformed report path (dummy-review-.md with empty timestamp). Affected files: orchestration/RULES.md:176. Fix: Add explicit variable assignment in Step 3b-i: TIMESTAMP=$(date +%Y%m%d-%H%M%S) and add cross-reference in Step 3b-v. Acceptance criteria: (1) Step 3b-i explicitly assigns timestamp to a named variable. (2) Step 3b-v TIMESTAMP reference resolves correctly. Found by: Clarity (CL-2 P2), Edge Cases (EC-4b P2), Excellence (EX-6 P2).

title: pantry.md Section 2 circular reference fix incomplete: Big Head Step 0/0a and polling loop not inlined
PLACEHOLDER_CONVENTIONS.md validation regex Pattern 4 has false negatives for mixed casing
scrub-pii.sh regex is overly broad (global replace, not field-scoped)
description: The PII scrub regex is applied globally across all content in issues.jsonl rather than being scoped to the known PII-bearing JSON fields (owner, created_by). Any email-like pattern in any field (titles, descriptions, URLs) would be silently scrubbed. Fix: scope the perl regex to target only owner and created_by field values. Consolidated from: Excellence F2.

title: compose-review-skeletons.sh sed regex converts all {UPPERCASE} tokens, not just slot markers
description:
Root cause: compose-review-skeletons.sh:99-102 uses `sed 's/{\([A-Z][A-Z_]*\)}/{{\1}}/g'` to convert single-brace placeholders to double-brace. This blanket regex would also convert any legitimate {UPPERCASE} text in template prose or code examples that is NOT a slot marker.

Additionally, the comment says 'WORD is all-caps with underscores' but the regex requires at least 2 characters ([A-Z][A-Z_]*), so a single-char placeholder like {X} would not match. The comment does not document this.

Affected surfaces:
- scripts/compose-review-skeletons.sh:99-102 (sed pattern and its comment)

Found by: Clarity (P3 - F8, comment accuracy), Edge Cases (P3 - F6, overly broad regex), Excellence (P3 - F3, maintainability)
3 reviewers found overlapping issues with the same sed line. Root cause is the same: a blanket regex with undocumented assumptions.

Fix: Either (a) use a whitelist of known slot names, or (b) add a comment documenting the assumption + fix the min-length documentation.

Acceptance criteria:
- [ ] Comment accurately describes the regex behavior (2+ char minimum)
- [ ] Either whitelist approach or documented assumption added

title: Big Head data file format split between pantry.md and reviews.md
description: pantry.md Step 4 lists what Big Head consolidation data should contain (4 report paths, dedup protocol, bead filing instructions, consolidated output path) but the actual format/structure is in reviews.md Big Head Consolidation Protocol section. A fresh Pantry would need to cross-reference but there's no explicit pointer. Add cross-reference from pantry.md Step 4 to reviews.md.

title: SETUP.md nested code fences break standard markdown rendering
AGG-042: Add archive exclusion warnings to prevent contamination
description:
orchestration/_archive/ contains older documents that match active template glob patterns. An agent using Glob with orchestration/**/*.md would read stale instructions contradicting current workflows. No DO NOT READ _archive warnings exist.

Fix: Add DO NOT READ files in orchestration/_archive/ to active templates Information Diet section. Consider renaming to _deprecated/.

Acceptance Criteria:
- [ ] RULES.md Information Diet section explicitly excludes orchestration/_archive/ from reads
- [ ] At least one other actively-searched template includes the _archive exclusion warning
- [ ] Agents following Information Diet rules will not read archived files via glob patterns
close_reason: Added _archive exclusion to RULES.md FORBIDDEN list and Information Diet, scout.md Step 1, and DO_NOT_READ.md sentinel

title: compose-review-skeletons.sh exit 1 in || block redundant with set -euo pipefail
Checkpoint thresholds undefined: small file, sampling N<3, CCB bd list unbounded
description: Root cause: checkpoints.md uses three undefined or broken thresholds: (1) CCO Check 7 uses 'small file' for WARN verdict but never defines what line count qualifies (line 82-84). Pre-existing bead ant-farm-jv4 tracks this. (2) DMVDC sampling formula max(3, min(5, ceil(N/3))) returns 3 when N=1 or N=2, but only 1-2 findings exist. Formula needs outer min(N, ...) guard (line 301). (3) CCB Check 7 runs bd list --status=open with no limit/scope, which can return thousands of items in mature repos. Fix: (1) Define small file = <100 lines. (2) Fix formula to min(N, max(3, min(5, ceil(N/3)))). (3) Scope CCB bead list to session-start date. Acceptance criteria: All three thresholds explicitly defined with concrete values. Found by: edge-cases-reviewer (P2 x2, P3 x1).

title: Pre-push hook blocks all git pushes when sync-to-claude.sh fails
description: Root cause: install-hooks.sh:34-45 generates a pre-push hook with set -euo pipefail that runs sync-to-claude.sh. If sync fails (rsync error, permission denied, disk full), set -e causes the hook to exit non-zero, blocking the git push. Developers cannot push code because of an unrelated sync failure. Fix: Wrap sync call in error handler that degrades to warning: 'if \! "$SYNC_SCRIPT"; then echo WARNING; fi'. Never block pushes for non-git reasons. Acceptance criteria: (1) Sync failure produces warning but push proceeds. (2) Sync success still works normally. Found by: edge-cases-reviewer (P2).

title: AGG-037: Clarify Big Head verification pipeline boundaries and gaps
description:
Both Big Head (Step 0) and Pest Control CCB (Check 0) verify all 4 Nitpicker reports exist. The redundancy is intentional but undocumented. Big Head also lacks verification that it actually read all reports.

Fix: Clarify: Big Head Step 0 is prerequisite (FAIL before reading), CCB Check 0 is audit (FAIL if skipped). Add read verification: After reading, confirm counts from each report.

Acceptance Criteria:
- [ ] reviews.md documents Big Head Step 0 as prerequisite and CCB Check 0 as audit with distinct descriptions
- [ ] Big Head output includes read confirmation with finding counts per report
- [ ] The two-layer verification design rationale is documented

title: Review task ID scoping includes out-of-range commits
description: Task ant-farm-1y4 was included in this review cycle but its fix commit predates the review range (f9ad7d9..HEAD). The reviewer cannot verify AC compliance. Fix: exclude tasks whose fix commits pre-date the session first commit when compiling review task IDs. Consolidated from: Correctness F2.

title: TASK_SUFFIX extraction algorithm uses first-hyphen logic but examples require last-hyphen
description: Root cause: The TASK_SUFFIX extraction algorithm in orchestration/reference/dependency-analysis.md:23-31 says 'Locate the first hyphen' and provides regex ^[^-]+-(.+)$ which both implement first-hyphen splitting. But the examples show last-hyphen splitting: ant-farm-9oa -> 9oa (not farm-9oa). The regex produces wrong suffixes for any project ID containing a hyphen in the project name (e.g., ant-farm-*). Wrong suffixes cascade to wrong artifact paths (pc-farm-9oa-cco-...md instead of pc-9oa-cco-...md), breaking DMVDC and CCB audits. Affected surfaces: dependency-analysis.md:23 (prose), dependency-analysis.md:25 (regex), dependency-analysis.md:28-29 (step-by-step algorithm). Fix: Change algorithm to use last hyphen, fix regex to ^.+-(.+)$. Acceptance criteria: (1) Algorithm prose says 'last hyphen'. (2) Regex is ^.+-(.+)$. (3) All examples still pass. (4) ant-farm-9oa -> 9oa verified. Found by: correctness-reviewer (P1), edge-cases-reviewer (P2), clarity-reviewer (P3).
close_reason: P1 fix: last-hyphen split algorithm applied across 6 files

title: rsync --delete in sync-to-claude.sh removes scripts/ dir on every sync
description:
Root cause: sync-to-claude.sh:23 uses `rsync -av --delete` to sync orchestration/ to ~/.claude/orchestration/. The scripts directory (~/.claude/orchestration/scripts/) is created by a later block (lines 27-33) from repo scripts/, NOT from orchestration/. The --delete flag removes anything in the target not in the source, so it deletes scripts/ on every run before the copy block recreates it. Currently works due to execution order, but fragile.

Affected surfaces:
- sync-to-claude.sh:23 (rsync --delete)
- sync-to-claude.sh:27-33 (script copy block depends on running AFTER rsync)

Found by: Excellence (P2), Edge Cases (P3)

Fix: Add `--exclude='scripts/'` to the rsync command, OR move compose-review-skeletons.sh and fill-review-slots.sh into orchestration/scripts/ in the repo.

Acceptance criteria:
- [ ] rsync does not delete ~/.claude/orchestration/scripts/ on subsequent runs
- [ ] Script sync works regardless of execution order within sync-to-claude.sh

title: Templates lack explicit guards for missing or empty input artifacts
description:
Root cause: Multiple templates assume their input artifacts exist and are well-formed without specifying explicit error behavior when they are missing or empty. This is a systematic gap across the template suite -- each template handles the happy path but does not specify what to write or return when a required input is absent.

Affected surfaces:
- pantry.md:26 -- No validation that task-metadata files exist before reading (Step 2)
- pantry.md:26-32 -- No handling for empty or malformed metadata fields (e.g., feature tasks with no 'Root cause')
- pantry.md:94 -- Review mode has no handling for empty changed-file list
- big-head-skeleton.md:23 -- Step 1 says 'FAIL immediately if any missing' but does not specify where to write a failure artifact
- checkpoints.md:259 -- No specification for handling bd show failures in Checkpoint B

Contributing reviews: Edge Cases (Findings 2, 3, 7, 8, 9 -- all P3)
All five findings share the same pattern: expected input artifact may be missing/empty, and the template does not specify explicit error behavior. P3 because each individual case is unlikely but the pattern is systemic.

Fix: Establish a convention across all templates: 'If an expected input artifact is missing or empty, write a brief failure artifact to the expected output path explaining the issue, then return FAIL with error details.' Apply this pattern to Pantry (missing/malformed metadata, empty file list), Big Head (missing reports), and Checkpoints (bd show failures).

Acceptance criteria:
- Each template has explicit instructions for handling missing/empty inputs
- Failure artifacts are written to expected output paths so downstream consumers are not left guessing
- Infrastructure failures (tool unavailability) are distinguished from substance failures (agent quality)

title: Scout strategy construction must verify all inventory tasks are assigned to agents
description:
During session 3be37d, the Scout listed 16 ready tasks in the inventory table but Strategy A only assigned 15 to agents. ant-farm-jv4 (checkpoint verdict thresholds, checkpoints.md) was listed in the inventory and file modification matrix but never placed in any agent grouping. The Queen caught the gap after Pantry composition but deferred instead of correcting before spawn.

Root cause: The Scout builds agent groupings file-first (what files does each hot file need?) rather than inventory-first (is every task accounted for?). Small, conflict-free tasks with no file overlaps are the most likely to be dropped since they don't trigger conflict-batching logic.

Fix: Add a mandatory verification step at the end of strategy construction in orchestration/templates/scout.md. After assigning all agents, count assigned tasks vs inventory count. If they differ, surface unassigned tasks explicitly before returning the briefing.

Acceptance Criteria:
- [ ] scout.md strategy construction section includes a post-assignment verification step
- [ ] The step compares assigned task count against inventory count and lists any unassigned tasks
- [ ] Unassigned tasks are flagged as errors that must be resolved before the briefing is returned

title: fix: WWD checkpoint skipped entirely in production session despite being documented as mandatory gate
description:
RULES.md:118-119 documents WWD (Wandering Worker Detection) as a mandatory hard gate: "after each agent commits, spawn Pest Control (model: haiku) for Wandering Worker Detection (WWD) (scope check before next agent in the wave can proceed)." checkpoints.md:264 echoes: "After agent commits, BEFORE spawning next agent in same wave." The Hard Gates table (RULES.md:259) lists WWD PASS as blocking "Next agent in wave."

Root cause: Two issues discovered in session artifact analysis:
1. In _session-7edaafbb (the largest observed session — 26 tasks, 7 parallel agents across multiple waves), zero WWD artifacts exist in the pc/ directory. The checkpoint was entirely skipped despite being documented as mandatory.
2. In _session-068ecc83 (smaller session), WWD did run but timing analysis shows all wave-1 agents committed within ~8 seconds of each other, with WWD artifacts appearing 47-78 seconds later in a batch. WWD was not actually gating individual agents — it was a post-hoc batch check.

The documentation describes per-agent serialized gating, but when agents run in parallel and commit nearly simultaneously, per-agent serialized gating is mechanically impossible. The docs do not acknowledge this reality. The known-failures reference (known-failures.md, Epic 74g) specifically cites WWD as the fix that would have caught a scope creep cascade — making its absence a real safety gap.

Current behavior: WWD is either skipped entirely (session-7edaafbb) or runs as a batch post-hoc check (session-068ecc83). Neither matches the documented "blocks next agent in wave" semantics.

Fix:
1. Investigate why _session-7edaafbb skipped WWD entirely — was it an intentional optimization for large waves, or a Queen process violation?
2. Update RULES.md Step 3 to document the actual behavior: when agents commit in parallel within a wave, WWD runs as a batch check after all commits rather than serializing individual agents
3. Define explicit criteria for when WWD can be batched vs when it must serialize (e.g., if agents share files, WWD must serialize; if agents have disjoint file sets, batch is acceptable)
4. Add a progress.log entry for WWD completion so skipped WWD is detectable during crash recovery

Changes needed:
- orchestration/RULES.md:118-119: Rewrite WWD timing description to match actual parallel-wave behavior
- orchestration/RULES.md Hard Gates table:259: Clarify "Next agent in wave" blocking semantics for parallel waves
- orchestration/templates/checkpoints.md:264: Update "When" field to distinguish serial vs batch WWD
- orchestration/RULES.md: Add progress.log entry for WWD phase (currently missing — no WWD_COMPLETE milestone)

Acceptance Criteria:
- [ ] RULES.md Step 3 accurately describes when WWD runs in batch vs serial mode
- [ ] Hard Gates table clarifies blocking semantics for parallel waves
- [ ] checkpoints.md WWD "When" field matches RULES.md description
- [ ] Progress log includes a WWD milestone entry (detectable in crash recovery)
- [ ] Next production session with parallel agents produces WWD artifacts (verified post-fix)

title: README.md architecture diagram does not capture Pest Control dual role
Pantry review mode has no fail-fast for empty changed-file list
New documents (GLOSSARY, PLACEHOLDER_CONVENTIONS, SESSION_PLAN) missing from README File Reference
bd show failure guard only on DMVDC Check 2, missing from other bd show callsites
description: The new bd show failure guard (ant-farm-zeu) is placed only on DMVDC Check 2 (checkpoints.md:332-337). However, other checkpoints also invoke bd show: WWD (line 245) and CCB Check 2 (lines 490-492). These callsites lack equivalent failure guards. If bd show can fail for DMVDC, it can fail for WWD and CCB too. Affected surfaces: checkpoints.md:245 (WWD), checkpoints.md:490-492 (CCB Check 2). Fix: Add similar guards to all bd show callsites, or create a shared 'bd show failure handling' note in the Pest Control Overview section.
